fields.description:
## Why
3183 (change 580, commit da27c5f30) delivered the merge-gate + snapshot ledger with a
divergent schema and verdict set vs. the APPROVED epic design (AC8/AC9 â€” the two ACs the
epic completion-verifier correctly FAILED on). This story reworks commit 7 to the approved
design AND performs the consumer cutover deferred from TS1 (in-toto payload) and TS2
(commit-ancestry era) â€” because the write-seam signing, the merge-gate, and the ledger all
live in THIS commit. Depends on TS1 (content_hash + in-toto Statement) and TS2
(resolve_event_commit + verify_authorship_at_commit). (User rejected the delivered design;
see session_log d7c0.)

## Stack scoping (why the cutover is here)
This story AMENDS Gerrit change 580 = commit da27c5f30 (3183), which introduces
`_seam.py`'s write-time signing, `verify_authorship.py` (the merge-gate), `compact.py`'s
ledger, `_config_schema.py`, and `_state.py`'s authorship summary. TS1 left the in-toto
`sign_event_authorship`/`verify_event_authorship` machinery in `authorship.py` (commit 3)
UNCONSUMED; TS2 removed the epoch API and added `resolve_event_commit` /
`verify_authorship_at_commit` (commit 4). At this commit those consumers currently still
call the REMOVED epoch API â€” so this story is REQUIRED to make the commit-7 patchset green,
and TS2+TS3 land together. `ensure_identity_for` (aliased by `create_placeholder`) lives in
commit 6, so `create_placeholder` also lands here (the lowest transition commit â‰Ą 6).

## What
- **Write-seam in-toto cutover (`_seam.py::_apply_authorship`):** stamp `author_sig` by
  calling `authorship.sign_event_authorship(event, signing_key, principal=author_id)` (the
  in-toto Statement envelope) instead of `sign_authorship(raw_canonical_bytes, ...)`.
- **Merge-gate rewire (`verify_authorship.py`):**
  - LIVE events: parse the DSSE payload as an in-toto Statement and check its subject binds
    the event (`subject[0].digest.sha256 == authorship_content_hash(ev)` and
    `subject[0].name == ev['uuid']`) instead of the raw-bytes `envelope.payload != ev.payload`
    compare; resolve the event's introducing commit via
    `authorship.resolve_event_commit(position, ticket_dir)` and verify with
    `authorship.verify_authorship_at_commit(envelope, author_id, event_commit, position)`
    instead of `epoch_for_position` + `verify_authorship_at_epoch`.
  - **Four-way classification with the new `key_not_valid_at_era`:**
    - `unsigned` â€” no `author_sig`.
    - `unknown-author` â€” no `author_id` / not an identity ticket.
    - `verified` â€” content binds AND the signature verifies against a key VALID at the
      event's commit (`verify_authorship_at_commit` verified).
    - `key_not_valid_at_era` â€” content binds AND the signature is cryptographically valid
      against SOME key the identity holds in its keyring (valid at some era) BUT that key was
      NOT valid at the event's commit (added later or already revoked). Distinguished from
      `bad-signature` by verifying against the identity's FULL historical keyring (a new
      `authorship.verify_authorship_any_key(envelope, identity_id)` helper that builds the
      trust root from ALL keyring public keys regardless of validity window): valid-any-key
      + not-valid-at-era â‡’ `key_not_valid_at_era`.
    - `bad-signature` â€” content mismatch, malformed envelope, or the signature verifies
      against NO key the identity ever held (forged).
- **Ledger (`compact.py::_build_authorship_ledger`):** record
  `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature, signer_pubkey, position}` per signed folded event,
  where `content_hash` is TS1's digest (from the event's Statement / recomputed),
  `signer_pubkey` is the actual public key that verified the signature (so a compacted event
  re-verifies WITHOUT scanning the keyring to find the key), and `position` is
  `{commit_sha, intra_commit_index}` â€” `commit_sha` resolved via
  `authorship.resolve_event_commit` at compaction time, `intra_commit_index` the event's
  ordinal within its commit. Replaces the delivered `{event_uuid, author_id, author_sig,
  epoch}`.
- **Ledger verification (`verify_authorship.py`):** for a ledger entry, crypto-verify the
  signature against the recorded `signer_pubkey` (a one-key trust root), check the Statement
  binds `content_hash`, and decide era from `position.commit_sha` against the SURVIVING
  snapshot keyring (retired raw KEY files are folded into the same snapshot) â€” emitting the
  same four-way verdict. Self-contained: no raw event file needed.
- **Verdict enum + display grouping (`verify_signature_result.schema.json`):** add
  `key_not_valid_at_era` to the `verdict` enum. Document the display grouping:
  `verified` / `unverified` (= `bad-signature` | `key_not_valid_at_era` | `unknown-author`)
  / `unsigned`.
- **`create_placeholder` (`identity.py`):** add the epic-AC-named
  `create_placeholder(provider, external_id, display_name, *, repo_root=None)` as a thin
  idempotent alias over the delivered `ensure_identity_for` (restores the approved public
  surface; mandated by epic AC1/the inbound-ghost AC).

## Scope
In: the write-seam in-toto cutover, the merge-gate rewire to commit-ancestry + in-toto
content-binding, the four-way verdict with `key_not_valid_at_era`, the approved ledger
schema + self-contained ledger verification, the schema enum + display grouping, and the
`create_placeholder` alias. Out: authorship payload machinery (TS1, done); era model
machinery (TS2, done); docs/ADR (TS4).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A live signed event round-trips: with write-time signing enabled, an event written by
  an identity with a valid key is classified `verified` by `rebar verify-authorship` (proves
  the `_seam` in-toto stamping + the merge-gate in-toto content-binding + commit-ancestry
  verify are wired end-to-end).
- [ ] The SNAPSHOT authorship ledger records `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature,
  signer_pubkey, position}` (position = `{commit_sha, intra_commit_index}`) per signed
  folded event; a round-trip test signs events, compacts, and asserts `verify-authorship`
  returns `verified` from the ledger ALONE (raw event files retired).
- [ ] `verify-authorship` emits a distinct `key_not_valid_at_era` for an event whose
  signature is cryptographically valid (by a key the identity holds) but whose key was NOT
  valid at the event's commit (e.g. signed by a since-revoked key), distinct from
  `bad-signature` (forged / no matching key) and `unknown-author` (no identity).
- [ ] `bad-signature` is still emitted for a genuinely forged signature (verifies against no
  key the identity ever held) and for a content-binding mismatch (test).
- [ ] `verify_signature_result.schema.json`'s `verdict` enum includes `key_not_valid_at_era`
  (test asserts the enum), and the `verified/unverified/unsigned` display grouping is
  documented.
- [ ] `create_placeholder(provider, external_id, display_name)` mints a keyless placeholder
  identity and is idempotent on repeat (returns the same identity), a thin alias over
  `ensure_identity_for` (test).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py` (stamp author_sig via sign_event_authorship â€” in-toto cutover)
- `src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` (in-toto content-binding + verify_authorship_at_commit + key_not_valid_at_era four-way verdict; read new ledger)
- `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` (approved ledger schema {content_hash,signature,signer_pubkey,position})
- `src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json` (add key_not_valid_at_era to enum)
- `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py` (add verify_authorship_any_key helper for the era/forged distinction)
- `src/rebar/_commands/identity.py` (create_placeholder alias over ensure_identity_for)
